content: For a brand-new review, the Codex thread/session ID does not exist until after the first SDK call returns. Any session-tracking logic that needs to activate state pre-flight only works for RESUMED sessions where the ID is passed in. New-review code must defer session activation until after the client call completes, then record the returned session_id.
context: Discovered building the storage layer — 2026-02-19 handover. Shaped the session lifecycle in src/storage/session-tracker.ts: sessions transition in_progress → completed/failed, but the in_progress row is only inserted after the SDK returns a thread ID. Relevant when fixing T-001 (multi-chunk orphaning) because the partial-failure path needs to use the real thread ID, not a placeholder.
title: Codex session ID is unknowable before the first API call
